# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { createClient } from '@vercel/edge-config';

interface EdgeConfig {
  apiKey?: string;
  dbUrl?: string;
  featureFlags?: {
    isEnabled?: boolean;
  };
}

const DEFAULTS = {
  apiKey: 'vercel_default_key_789',
  dbUrl: 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com',
  featureFlags: {
    isEnabled: false,
  },
};

export default async function handler(request: Request) {
  const edgeConfig = process.env.EDGE_CONFIG;
  
  if (!edgeConfig) {
    throw new Error('EDGE_CONFIG environment variable is not set');
  }

  const client = createClient(edgeConfig);
  
  let config: EdgeConfig = {};
  
  try {
    config = await client.get<EdgeConfig>();
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Failed to fetch Edge Config:', error);
  }

  const apiKey = config.apiKey || DEFAULTS.apiKey;
  const dbUrl = config.dbUrl || DEFAULTS.dbUrl;
  const featureFlag = config.featureFlags?.isEnabled ?? DEFAULTS.featureFlags.isEnabled;

  // Validate required values
  if (!apiKey || !dbUrl) {
    throw new Error('Required configuration values are missing');
  }

  // Use the configuration values
  return new Response(
    JSON.stringify({
      apiKey: apiKey,
      dbUrl: dbUrl,
      featureFlag: featureFlag,
    }),
    {
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    }
  );
}

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};
